---
title: "Chapter N — Topic"
subtitle: "Template slide deck (Python ↔ R)"
format:
  html: default
  revealjs:
    output-file: template-slides.html
---

# Section heading {.section}

A section divider slide. Replace with the section title for the chapter
this deck covers.

## A regular slide

Regular content slides start at H2.

- Bullet
- Bullet
- Bullet

## Python ↔ R code, side-by-side

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Python

```python
# Replace this with the example for the chapter the deck covers.
import numpy as np
x = np.arange(1, 6)
x.mean()
```

### R

```r
# Replace this with the R equivalent.
x <- 1:5
mean(x)
```

:::

<!--
Author note (not rendered):

The code blocks above use plain markdown fences (```python, ```r) on
purpose, NOT Quarto execution chunks (```{python}, ```{r}). Plain fences
are pure syntax highlighting — no Python or R engine is invoked, so the
preview / publish workflows do not need Python or R installed. When
chapter decks eventually run real code, switch to ```{python} / ```{r}
chunks AND add `setup-python` (and `r-lib/actions/setup-r` for R) steps
to .github/workflows/{publish,preview}.yml, plus a `requirements.txt`
and (for R) a `renv.lock` to pin versions.
-->

## A second tabset slide

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Python

```python
# Example 2
```

### R

```r
# Example 2
```

:::

<!--
Author note: prose written after the tabset's closing ::: and before
the next H2 will render INSIDE the previous slide in revealjs (and
inside the previous section in html). To put commentary on its own
slide, use a fresh ## heading. To document something for the .qmd
author only, use an HTML comment like this one.
-->

## How to add a new chapter deck

1. Copy `template.qmd` to `slides/ChNN-<topic>.qmd`.
2. Update the YAML `title`, `subtitle`, and
   `revealjs.output-file: ChNN-<topic>-slides.html`.
3. Replace the template slides with chapter content (subject to the IP
   discussion in `slides/index.qmd`).
4. Add the deck to the navbar in the project root `_quarto.yml`.
